subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Board side split** — Imperium is for **3-5 players**.
- **Board side split** — Italia is for **2-4 players**.
- **Market filtering** — For-sale cards not needed by player count are removed.
- **Starting deck scaling** — 35 starting cards are organized as **7 per player** (decks I-V).

#### Boards and Reference Items
- **Game board** — 1 double-sided board (Imperium/Italia).
- **Storehouses** — 5 storehouses.
- **Player aids** — 5 player aids.
- **Rules materials** — 1 game rules, 1 quick intro, 1 historical booklet.

#### Wooden Pieces and Goods
- **Wooden player pieces** — 110 total in 5 colors.
- **Per-player set** — 3 sea colonists, 3 land colonists, 1 scoring marker, 15 houses.
- **Goods units** — 80 units (brick, food, tool, wine, cloth).
- **City tokens** — 30 tokens.
- **Bonus markers** — 24 markers.

#### Cards and Special Marker
- **Total cards** — 72 cards.
- **Personality cards** — 65 cards.
- **Starting cards** — 35 cards (7 per player).
- **Cards for sale** — 30 cards.
- **Concordia card** — 1 card.
- **Praefectus Magnus card** — 1 card.

#### Coins
- **Coin denominations** — 1, 2, 5, and 10 sestertii.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Choose map side** — Pick Imperium/Italia based on player count.
2. **Place map pieces** — Place city tokens and province bonus markers per quick intro.
3. **Create market display** — Put 7 personality cards face up.
4. **Filter sale cards** — Remove cards not used at this player count.
5. **Set market stack** — Put remaining for-sale cards as draw stack.
6. **Place supplies** — Put goods and sestertii bank within reach.
7. **Place Concordia card** — Keep available for end-triggering player.
8. **Choose start player** — Determine randomly.
9. **Assign Praefectus Magnus** — Give to player on start player's right.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2 Players ---
- **Map side** — Use Italia side (2-player map recommendation).
--- 3 Players ---
- **Map side** — Use Imperium side for 3-player standard setup.
--- 4 Players ---
- **Map side** — Use either Imperium or Italia for 4 players (2 legal maps).
--- 5 Players ---
- **Map side** — Use Imperium side for 5-player setup.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Personal Setup
- **Color selection** — Each player takes one full color set.
- **Storehouse** — Each player takes 1 storehouse.
- **House supply** — Each player starts with 15 houses in reserve.
- **Colonist supply** — Each player has 3 sea + 3 land colonists in set.

#### Starting Hand
- **Hand size** — Each player gets 7 starting personality cards.
- **Deck source** — Starting hands come from decks I-V (7 per player structure).

#### Starting Position
- **Initial placements** — Follow quick intro for map-specific starting placements.
- **Roma reference** — New colonists bought by Tribune enter in Roma.

- **Starting cards** — 7 per player.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start Conditions
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise from random start player.
- **Praefectus holder** — Player to start player's right gets Praefectus Magnus.
- **Turn rule** — On your turn, play exactly 1 personality card from hand.
- **Discard visibility** — Played cards go to personal discard pile with top card visible.
- **Market open** — 7 cards are available in display at game start.

#### End Trigger Reminder
- **Trigger A** — End can begin when last display card is purchased.
- **Trigger B** — End can begin when first player builds 15th house.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Continuous Turn Loop
1. **Play card** — Choose and play exactly 1 personality card from your hand.
2. **Resolve action** — Resolve that card fully.
3. **Discard card** — Put it on your personal discard pile.
4. **Pass turn** — Next player clockwise takes their turn.

#### Hand Cycle
- **Action menu** — Your hand is your action menu.
- **Reset tool** — Tribune recovers played cards back to hand.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Tribune
- **Recover cards** — Take back all previously played cards.
- **Coin bonus** — Gain 1 sestertius per recovered card above 3 (including Tribune).
- **Optional colonist** — Pay 1 food + 1 tool to place 1 new land/sea colonist in Roma.

#### Architect
- **Move allowance** — Total move steps equals your colonists on board.
- **Route limits** — Land uses brown routes; sea uses blue routes.
- **Split movement** — You may split steps across colonists.
- **Endpoint restriction** — Cannot end on occupied route segment.
- **Build timing** — Build only after all movement finishes.
- **Build location** — Build in cities adjacent to your colonists.
- **Goods cost (brick city)** — 1 food.
- **Goods cost (other cities)** — 1 brick + city good type.
- **Coin base costs** — Brick 1, Food 2, Tool 3, Wine 4, Cloth 5.
- **Crowding multiplier** — Multiply coin cost by total houses after build.
- **Build limits** — Max one own house per city; never in Roma.

#### Prefect
- **Mode A (produce)** — Choose active province with goods-side marker.
- **Province bonus** — Gain 1 bonus good from chosen province marker.
- **Province flip** — Flip that marker to coin side.
- **House production** — All houses in province produce their city goods.
- **Mode B (cash bonus)** — Gain 1 sestertius per visible coin on province markers.
- **Reactivate provinces** — Flip all coin-side markers back to goods side.

#### Colonist
- **Mode A (place colonists)** — Pay 1 food + 1 tool per colonist placed.
- **Placement options** — Place in Roma or city where you own a house.
- **Mode B (take cash)** — Gain 5 sestertii + 1 per own colonist on board.

#### Mercator
- **Cash gain** — Gain 3 sestertii (or 5 with purchased Mercator).
- **Trade cap** — Trade up to 2 goods types with bank.
- **Quantity limits** — Limited by money and free storage spaces.

#### Diplomat
- **Copy action** — Use top face-up personality card from another player's discard pile.
- **Copy limit** — Cannot copy players who just used Diplomat or just recovered via Tribune.

#### Senator
- **Buy count** — Buy up to 2 cards from display.
- **Cost formula** — Red-box cost + slot cost below card position.
- **Question-mark slot** — "?" means any one good.
- **Refill** — Shift left and refill to 7 while stack exists.

#### Consul
- **Buy count** — Buy exactly 1 display card.
- **Cost formula** — Pay only red-box cost.
- **Slot costs ignored** — Ignore goods printed below position.
- **Refill** — Shift left and refill to 7 while stack exists.

#### Specialists (Mason/Farmer/Smith/Vintner/Weaver)
- **Production effect** — All your houses of matching type produce 1 each.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Praefectus Magnus
- **Double bonus** — Holder gets 2 province bonus goods when using Prefect production mode.
- **Scope** — City production is unchanged by this doubling.
- **Pass rule** — After use, pass Praefectus Magnus to player on holder's right.
- **Mandatory use** — Holder must use it when able; cannot save for later.

#### Province-Cycle Economy
- **Active state** — Goods-side marker = active province for production mode.
- **Spent state** — Produced province flips to coin side.
- **Reset state** — Cash-bonus Prefect flips all spent markers back to goods side.

#### Movement/Blocking
- **Blocking unit** — Route segment occupancy matters, not city occupancy.
- **Pass-through** — You may pass through occupied routes but cannot end there.

#### Cost Pressure
- **Coin escalation** — Coin costs increase with city crowding.
- **Goods stability** — Goods costs do not multiply with crowding.

#### Market Compression
- **Immediate shift** — Purchased gaps collapse left immediately.
- **Timing edge** — Position shifts change effective affordability over time.

subtopics.title: God-Based Card Scoring Reference
subtopics.content:
#### Core Principle
- **Scoring basis** — Final VP is based on personality cards and their gods.
- **Achievement links** — Gods reward specific achievements (examples in rule text include populated provinces and colonist count).
- **Deck impact** — Card purchases are both action upgrades and scoring multipliers.

#### Practical Use
- **Alignment rule** — Build board state that matches your god mix.
- **Purchase discipline** — Prioritize cards that improve both tempo and final-scoring fit.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Triggers
- **Trigger A** — A player buys the last card from display.
- **Trigger B** — A player builds their 15th house.
- **Concordia card** — Triggering player immediately takes Concordia card.
- **Final turns** — All other players take one final turn.

#### Final Scoring
- **God-card scoring** — Score all personality cards by god criteria.
- **Examples from rules text** — Criteria include populated provinces and colonists.
- **Concordia scoring** — Include Concordia card value/benefit.
- **Winner** — Highest total VP wins.

#### Tiebreakers
- **Tiebreak 1** — Most sestertii wins.
- **Tiebreak 2** — If tied, higher total goods value in storehouse wins.
- **Final tie** — Shared victory if still tied.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Priorities
- **Route spread first** — Use early Architect turns to reach multiple build-adjacent cities.
- **Economy triangle** — Build balance across cash flow, goods flow, and hand-cycle timing.
- **Scoring lane commit** — Choose at least two compatible god-scoring directions early.
- **Market efficiency** — Compare Senator slot-tax efficiency vs Consul single-buy efficiency each turn.
- **Prefect timing** — Choose produce vs cash based on current marker states, not habit.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Mistakes
- **Late Tribune** — Delaying resets causes low-value turns and weaker cycle tempo.
- **Crowded-city overpay** — Entering expensive contested cities without plan drains cash curve.
- **Storage neglect** — Ignoring storehouse space wastes trade/production opportunities.
- **Action-only card buys** — Buying cards for text alone can weaken final god-score multiplier mix.
- **Poor Prefect sequencing** — Mistimed cash reset can benefit opponents more than you.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision A: Houses vs Cards
- **Board line** — More houses improve production web and map access.
- **Deck line** — More personality cards improve action breadth and scoring multipliers.

#### Decision B: Tribune Timing
- **Reset now** — Recover hand to restore strong action options and coin bonus timing.
- **Delay reset** — Squeeze one tactical turn only if current board state strongly rewards it.

#### Decision C: Prefect Mode
- **Produce mode** — Gain immediate goods and province-wide production.
- **Cash mode** — Take coin burst and reactivate all spent provinces.

#### Decision D: End Trigger Control
- **Trigger early** — End game when your scoring conversion is already locked.
- **Delay end** — Extend only if extra cycle improves your score more than opponents'.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first readability with explicit Step 0 player-count audit and exact per-count headers from provided Concordia rule text.
